note: Deterministic, offline eval corpus. Each entry pairs a saved rendered-DOM snapshot with hand-authored ground truth: `truth` is the full main-content text that SHOULD survive extraction (used for word-level precision/recall/F1), `forbidden` is noise that must NOT appear. `type` buckets the page so the harness can break scores out per page type. The article/blog/docs/forum/newsletter/recipe entries reuse the test fixtures (synthetic, authored to Mantis's contract); the product/spa/amp/paywall entries are deliberately adversarial fixtures authored to stress known failure modes, NOT to flatter Mantis. None are real wild-web captures yet (see eval/README.md).

entries.name: chrome-heavy article
entries.truth: The Useful Essay. Alpha article paragraph is central to the story and contains enough prose to be scored as article content. Bravo article paragraph continues the argument with additional detail that should be extracted. Charlie quoted passage belongs to the article and should be retained with the main text.

entries.name: docs page
entries.truth: Install the Widget. November docs paragraph explains the setup process and belongs in the extracted content. Configure access. Oscar docs paragraph describes configuration tradeoffs and belongs in the extracted content. widget configure --project mantis --mode capture

entries.name: recipe page
entries.truth: Paneer Rice Bowl. Papa recipe introduction explains why the dish works and should be retained. Quebec recipe paragraph covers substitutions and timing and should be retained. Ingredients. one cup rice with enough item text to be useful. two cups paneer with enough item text to be useful.

entries.name: forum answer
entries.truth: Forum Thread. Romeo forum answer contains the substantive explanation and should be extracted. Sierra forum answer continues with practical detail and should be extracted.

entries.name: medium-like article
entries.truth: Medium Layout Story. Juliet medium paragraph carries the first core idea and should be extracted. Kilo medium paragraph carries the second core idea and should be extracted.

entries.name: substack-like newsletter
entries.truth: A Letter From the Future. Hotel newsletter paragraph opens the essay with content that should be captured. India newsletter paragraph expands the argument and should remain in the result.

entries.name: semantic metadata article
entries.truth: Semantic Headline. Foxtrot article paragraph survives surrounding tools and remains part of the main content. Golf article paragraph survives surrounding tools and provides another extraction signal.

entries.name: responsive duplicate article
entries.truth: Responsive Duplicate. Lima responsive paragraph should appear only once after duplicate body content is normalized. Mike responsive paragraph should appear only once after duplicate body content is normalized.

entries.name: hidden template article
entries.truth: Visible Article. Delta visible paragraph should be captured because it is part of the rendered article. Echo visible paragraph should also be captured because it continues the rendered article.

entries.name: title cleanup article
entries.truth: Tango title cleanup paragraph anchors the page content and should be extracted. Uniform title cleanup paragraph gives the extractor another content signal.

entries.name: link rich article
entries.truth: Link Rich Story. Victor link paragraph cites a primary source while remaining readable article text. Whiskey link paragraph points to supporting context without becoming navigation. Yankee link paragraph keeps the article above the short content threshold.

entries.name: product page (adversarial)
entries.truth: AeroPress Go Travel Coffee Press. The AeroPress Go brews smooth, full-bodied coffee without bitterness and packs into its own mug for travel. It makes one to three cups in about a minute and cleans up in seconds. Designed for camping, commuting, and small kitchens, it weighs under half a pound and needs no electricity, only hot water and a paper micro-filter.

entries.name: spa shell (adversarial)
entries.truth: Why we rewrote our scheduler. Our original scheduler was a single cron process that fanned out jobs to workers, and it served us well until the queue depth crossed a few million tasks a day. The rewrite splits scheduling from execution: a planner decides what should run and when, while a separate fleet of executors pulls work and reports completion back through an idempotent ledger. The hard part was not the code but the migration, because we had to run both systems in parallel and prove the new one matched the old one task for task before cutting over.
forbidden: Read more from our engineering team

entries.name: amp article (adversarial)
entries.truth: Coastal towns brace for autumn storms. Residents along the eastern coast spent the weekend stacking sandbags as forecasters warned of the strongest storm system to approach the region in over a decade. Emergency services opened shelters in three counties and urged people in low-lying areas to move to higher ground before the tide peaks on Tuesday evening. Power companies pre-positioned crews inland, saying they expect outages to last several days once winds exceed sixty miles per hour across the exposed headlands.

entries.name: paywall article (adversarial)
entries.truth: The quiet economics of small ferries. Small passenger ferries rarely turn a profit on fares alone, yet hundreds of them keep running across bays and rivers that bridges never reached. The math only works because the boats are treated as roads: subsidized as public infrastructure rather than judged as standalone businesses with their own balance sheets.
